title: Write 'How to Add a New Design Pattern' contributor guide
description: DX AUDIT: CONTRIBUTING.md covers System Design nodes only — zero LLD guidance. A contributor wanting to add Abstract Factory (LLD-091) has no documentation. Create a step-by-step guide: (1) Copy an existing pattern in patterns.ts as template, (2) Fill all required fields (with field-by-field explanation), (3) Add UML classes with x/y positioning guidelines, (4) Write TS+Python code samples, (5) Test in dev mode, (6) Quality checklist. Include a worked example adding a complete pattern from scratch. Saves ~30 min per new contributor.
acceptanceCriteria: Guide at docs/guides/add-new-pattern.md or in CONTRIBUTING.md under LLD section
Step-by-step instructions with exact file paths
Annotated template showing every DesignPattern field with explanation
x/y positioning guidelines for UML class layout
Quality checklist: hook? analogy? 3 examples? TS+Python code? whenToUse?
Worked example: adding Abstract Factory from scratch

title: Create pattern scaffolding script — pnpm scaffold:pattern
description: DX AUDIT: No scaffolding exists. Adding a pattern requires: copy-pasting ~100 lines of nested objects, remembering all required fields, manually setting x/y positions, writing both TS+Python code. Create a Node.js script at scripts/scaffold-pattern.ts that: (1) accepts pattern name + category, (2) generates DesignPattern object with all required fields as TODO placeholders, (3) auto-generates deterministic IDs, (4) provides default x/y positions in a grid layout, (5) outputs to console for copy-paste or appends to patterns.ts. Saves ~15 min per pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: pnpm scaffold:pattern 'Abstract Factory' --category creational works
Generates complete DesignPattern object with all 10+ fields
All fields have placeholder values (TODO comments for content)
Auto-generates class IDs and relationship IDs
Provides default grid-layout x/y positions for 4-6 classes
Output is valid TypeScript that compiles without errors
README in docs/guides/ explains usage

title: Add JSDoc documentation to all exported functions in lib/lld/
description: DX AUDIT: bidirectional-sync.ts has excellent JSDoc (SyncManager class doc with usage example at line 183-191). But most other files have minimal or no JSDoc: patterns.ts exports (getPatternById, getPatternsByCategory) have zero doc. class-diagram-model.ts has one-line doc per function. codegen files have some doc but inconsistent. Add JSDoc to ALL exported functions with: purpose, parameters, return type, usage example for complex functions.
acceptanceCriteria: Every exported function in lib/lld/ has JSDoc with @param and @returns
Complex functions (SyncManager methods, parseTypeScript, generateTypeScript) have @example
barrel export index.ts has module-level JSDoc comment explaining the library
IDE hover shows documentation for every import from @/lib/lld

title: Add named constants for magic numbers in LLDModule.tsx and simulators
description: DX AUDIT: Multiple magic numbers without explanation: CLASS_BOX_WIDTH=220 (line 95, why?), ROW_HEIGHT=18 (line 97, not on 4px grid), interval 400ms (PatternBehavioralSimulator.tsx:252), max requests 100 (line 254), SEQ_PARTICIPANT_GAP=180 (line 1855). Add comments explaining WHY each value was chosen, or extract to named constants with descriptive names. E.g.: `const SIMULATION_TICK_MS = 400; // Balances visual speed with comprehension`.
acceptanceCriteria: All magic numbers in LLDModule.tsx have either a comment explaining WHY or are extracted to named constants
Constants have names that explain purpose (SIMULATION_TICK_MS, MAX_SIMULATION_REQUESTS)
ROW_HEIGHT changed to 20 (on 4px grid) or documented why 18 is intentional
PatternBehavioralSimulator magic numbers documented

title: Write LLD module architecture overview with data flow diagram
description: DX AUDIT: No architecture documentation exists for LLD. A new developer faces: 3463-line LLDModule.tsx, 2669-line patterns.ts, 6 codegen files, bidirectional sync engine, and 2 sub-components with zero overview of how they connect. Create: Mermaid data flow diagram showing User Action → Hook → State → Canvas/Properties/BottomPanel. Component tree diagram. File responsibility map. 'Where is X?' quick reference.
acceptanceCriteria: Architecture doc at docs/architecture/lld-module.md
Mermaid data flow diagram: user action → useLLDModule hook → state → UI panels
Component tree showing all 27+ components and their relationships
File responsibility table: 'Looking for X? Check Y file at line Z'
'Where is X?' quick reference for top 10 common questions

title: Add developer-facing error messages for common LLD contribution mistakes
description: DX AUDIT: When a contributor makes a mistake (duplicate class name, invalid stereotype, missing relationship target), feedback ranges from silent failure to cryptic runtime error. Add development-mode validation: (1) validateDiagram() at class-diagram-model.ts:182 already validates — wire it to run on pattern load in dev mode and console.warn results, (2) Add pattern content validation that checks all required fields, (3) Log helpful messages: 'Pattern "X" has relationship referencing non-existent class "Y". Check patterns.ts.'
acceptanceCriteria: Dev mode: validateDiagram() runs on every pattern load and console.warns issues
Dev mode: content validation checks all required fields on module init
Warning format: 'LLD Content Warning: Pattern "X" — [specific issue]. Fix in patterns.ts.'
Validation disabled in production (no performance impact)
At least 5 common mistakes detected: missing class ID, invalid stereotype, orphan relationship, empty description, missing code sample
